strengths: Roughly 19 years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, desktop and embedded systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq
Founder of GISCollective and a track record of public talks and workshops, showing initiative and knowledge-sharing
concerns: Career has plateaued at Senior/Staff IC level with no evidence of Director-level scope, cross-org strategy ownership, or advising strategic customers and partners
AI credentials are thin and recent: ~1 year of agent tooling work, no advanced AI degree, and no recognized AI expertise or demonstrated AI products/platforms at scale
No entrepreneurial experience inside a larger organization; background is small companies and a niche open-source side project, with no cloud-platform strategy depth beyond AWS CDK usage

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs Director-level Principal role
Shallow, recent AI expertise without recognition or shipped AI platforms

response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile hands-on engineer, but the role demands a Director-level individual contributor who shapes cloud and AI strategy, advises strategic customers, and carries recognized AI authority. His resume shows no experience operating at that altitude: titles top out at Senior/Staff, AI involvement is limited to about a year of agent tooling with no advanced AI education, publications, or flagship AI products, and his entrepreneurial work is a small open-source mapping platform rather than intrapreneurship within a large organization. Strong builder fluency does not compensate for the missing strategic leadership and AI standing, making him a weak fit for this Principal Engineer position.
